notes: Round-3 labeling.py ablation #5: same Platt consumption as sub33_label_platt, paired with an OUT-OF-DISTRIBUTION-DIVERSITY acquisition_function (score = -cos_sim(item_emb, train_centroid)). Items whose text is FURTHEST from the mean training item embedding get the highest score. Hypothesis: OOD items are exactly where sub 33's kNN fails (cold-start by similarity), so OOD-diversity labeling should help Platt more than uncertainty if item-text coverage was the bottleneck.
